title: Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024
subtitle: Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to [national estimates](#dod:ilo-national-estimates) and may not be fully comparable across countries.
caption:
Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024
Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to national estimates and may not be fully comparable across countries.

This scatter plot compares female employment rates across countries in 1980 versus 2024, with each circle representing a nation sized by population. The horizontal axis measures the 2024 rate from 0% to 100%, while the vertical axis measures the 1980 rate over the same range. Employment is defined in line with the 13th ICLS and includes the production of goods for own use, such as subsistence farming. Most labeled countries sit below the diagonal line where 1980 and 2024 values would be equal, showing that 2024 rates exceed 1980 rates for these nations. The United States, Brazil, and the United Kingdom cluster in the middle of the chart, with 2024 rates between 50% and 60% and 1980 rates between 40% and 50%. Thailand and Burkina Faso show high 1980 rates around 70% to 80%, with 2024 rates near 60%. Malawi stands out with a 1980 rate near 90% and a 2024 rate around 35%. At the lower end, Pakistan and Bangladesh have 2024 rates between 20% and 40% and 1980 rates below 15%. Nigeria and the Bahamas appear on the far right, with 2024 rates between 75% and 90% and 1980 rates between 25% and 35%. The color-coded legend groups countries by region: North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. The largest circles correspond to populous nations like the United States, Brazil, and Nigeria. The dominant pattern shows that for most labeled countries, the 2024 female employment rate is higher than the 1980 rate, with a few exceptions where the 1980 rate remains higher.
